prompt: Why do deep search agents need evidence-sensitive rewards beyond outcome-only task success?
reference_answer: Deep search agents need evidence-sensitive rewards because long-horizon search involves many steps and tool calls, so binary success signals are too sparse and can reward shallow shortcuts. ASearcher shows that search can go far beyond ten turns and over 100 tool calls, while DeepDive combines knowledge-graph data synthesis with multi-turn RL for hard search. CaRR directly argues that binary outcome reward misses comprehensiveness and factuality, and introduces citation-aware rubric rewards to encourage evidence-grounded reasoning.

documents.title: ASearcher digest
documents.content: ASearcher says long-horizon deep search can require many turns and tool calls, sometimes beyond one hundred, which makes outcome-only supervision sparse. The project scales asynchronous RL to long-horizon search so those longer trajectories become trainable.

documents.title: DeepDive digest
documents.content: DeepDive trains deep search agents with automated data synthesis from knowledge graphs and end-to-end multi-turn reinforcement learning. It explicitly targets complex multi-step information-seeking tasks and longer reasoning paths.

documents.title: CaRR digest
documents.content: CaRR argues that binary outcome rewards for deep search agents miss comprehensiveness, factuality, and grounded reasoning quality. It introduces citation-aware rubric rewards and evidence-chain checks so the reward reflects citation grounding rather than only final correctness.

required_claims.text: Long-horizon deep search creates sparse supervision because agents may need many turns and tool calls before success is visible.
Outcome-only reward misses whether the reasoning is comprehensive and factually grounded.
Evidence-sensitive rewards such as citation-aware rubrics help reward citation grounding and evidence-chain quality rather than only final correctness.

provenance.refresh_hint: Refresh if deep-search reward framing or repository claims change.
notes: Anchored to deep-search RL and citation-aware reward sources.
